info.title: SocialSyncs Public API
info.description:
API for managing social media posts, integrations, and media uploads in SocialSyncs.

## Authentication

All endpoints require an API key passed in the `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: your-api-key
```

Get your API key from SocialSyncs Settings.

## Rate Limits

There is a limit of **30 requests per hour**.

## Terminology

The UI uses `channel`, but the API uses `integration`. They refer to the same thing.

## Supported Platforms (27)

**Social Platforms:** X (Twitter), LinkedIn, LinkedIn Page, Facebook, Instagram, Instagram Standalone, Threads, Bluesky, Mastodon, Warpcast (Farcaster), Nostr, VK

**Video Platforms:** YouTube, TikTok

**Community Platforms:** Reddit, Lemmy, Discord, Slack, Telegram

**Design Platforms:** Pinterest, Dribbble

**Blogging Platforms:** Medium, Dev.to, Hashnode, WordPress

**Business:** Google My Business (GMB), Listmonk (newsletters)

tags.name: Analytics
tags.description: View analytics for integrations and posts

get.description: Returns all connected social media channels for your organization.

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Filter integrations by customer (group) ID. Returns only channels assigned to the given group.

get.description: Returns all customers (groups) for your organization. Use a group ID to filter the integrations list.

delete.description: Delete a connected channel by its integration ID. Any scheduled posts associated with this channel will also be deleted.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Integration ID of the channel to delete

get.summary: Get OAuth URL for a channel
get.description: Generate an OAuth authorization URL for a given integration. Use this to connect a new social media channel. Only OAuth-based integrations are supported (integrations that require an external URL, such as Mastodon, are not available via this endpoint).

parameters.name: integration
parameters.description: The integration identifier (e.g. x, linkedin, facebook, instagram, youtube, tiktok, reddit, etc.)

parameters.name: refresh
parameters.description: Pass an existing integration ID to refresh its OAuth token instead of creating a new connection

url.description: The OAuth authorization URL to redirect the user to

400.description: Integration not allowed or requires an external URL

get.description: Returns the posting rules, maximum content length, settings schema, and available provider tools for a connected channel. Use this to discover which tools can be executed with the trigger endpoint.

200.description: Settings and tools for the integration

settings.description: JSON schema of the provider settings, or a string when no settings are required

tools.description: Provider tools that can be executed via the trigger endpoint

output.rules: Instagram should have at least one attachment, if it's a story, it can have only one picture

tools.description: Search audio (music or original sounds) to attach to a Reel via the "audio" setting, an empty query returns trending audio

dataSchema.description: Search query, leave empty for trending audio

post.description: Executes a provider-specific tool on a connected channel, for example searching Instagram audio for Reels, listing Discord channels, or fetching Reddit flairs. Discover available tools with the integration settings endpoint.

methodName.description: The tool method name, as returned by the integration settings endpoint

output.description: The tool result, shape depends on the tool

401.description: Channel disconnected due to expired token

get.description: Verify if your API key is valid and connected.
Get the next available time slot for posting to a specific channel.
Get posts within a date range.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start date in UTC ISO format

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End date in UTC ISO format

post.description: Create or schedule a new post. Each social media platform has its own settings schema.

general.summary: General (Threads, Mastodon, Bluesky, Telegram, Nostr, VK, Kick)

value.content: Check out our latest product showcase!
Company update: We just launched a new feature!

# My Article

This is my article content in markdown...

delete.description: Delete a post by its ID. The API will look up the post and delete all posts in the same group.
Delete all posts in a group by the group identifier.

get.description:
When a post has been published but the platform did not return a usable post ID (the `releaseId` is set to `"missing"`), this endpoint fetches recent content from the provider so you can match and connect the correct one to the post.

The provider must implement the optional `missing` method. If the provider does not support it, an empty array is returned.

200.description: List of recent content items from the provider

put.description:
Updates the `releaseId` of a post that currently has its release ID set to `"missing"`. This connects the post to its actual published content on the platform, enabling analytics and statistics tracking.

Typically used after calling the **Get Missing Content** endpoint to retrieve the list of available content items.

releaseId.description: The platform-specific content ID to connect to this post

put.description:
Moves a post between `draft` and `schedule` state.

- `schedule` → sets the post state to `QUEUE` and (re)starts the publishing workflow so it will be published at its stored date.
- `draft` → sets the post state to `DRAFT` and terminates any currently running publishing workflow for the post, so it will not be published.

The post keeps its stored date; only the state (and the workflow) changes.

400.description: Invalid status or post not found

get.description: Get analytics data for a specific integration/channel. Returns metrics like followers, impressions, engagement, etc. depending on the platform.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Number of days to look back for analytics data (e.g. 7, 30, 90)

get.description: Get analytics data for a specific published post. Returns metrics like likes, comments, shares, impressions, etc. depending on the platform.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Number of days to look back for analytics data (e.g. 7, 30, 90)

post.description: Upload a media file using multipart form data.
Upload a file from an existing URL.

url.description: URL of the file to upload

get.description: Get paginated notifications for your organization, sorted by most recent first. Returns 100 notifications per page.

notifications.content: Your post to X was published successfully
Failed to publish post to LinkedIn

post.description: Create AI-generated videos for your posts.

customParams.prompt: A scenic view of mountains at sunset

post.description: Execute video-related functions like loading available voices.

date.description: Publish date in UTC ISO format

who_can_reply_post.description: Who can reply to the post

__type.description: Use 'linkedin' for profiles, 'linkedin-page' for company pages

post_as_images_carousel.description: Display multiple images as a carousel

is_trial_reel.description: Whether to post as a trial reel

callToActionUrl.description: URL for CTA (required if callToActionType is set)

url.description: Optional link URL to include in the post

EmptySettings.title: Other Platforms (Threads, Mastodon, Bluesky, etc.)
EmptySettings.description: For platforms that don't require additional settings

hasMore.description: Whether there are more pages available

link.description: Optional link associated with the notification

url.description: Thumbnail or preview URL for the content

percentageChange.description: Percentage change over the selected period
